name: azure-keyvault-keys-ts
description: Manage cryptographic keys using Azure Key Vault Keys SDK for JavaScript (@azure/keyvault-keys). Use when creating, encrypting/decrypting, signing, or rotating keys.
capabilities: Create RSA and elliptic curve keys in Azure Key Vault
Encrypt and decrypt data using Azure Key Vault cryptography clients
Sign and verify digital signatures with managed keys
Rotate keys manually or automatically with rotation policies
Backup and restore keys across vaults
List, get, and delete keys with proper error handling
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 47 potential issues, but all are false positives. The file is legitimate Azure SDK documentation containing example code blocks (not actual executables), placeholder URLs (not real endpoints), and standard environment variable patterns (Azure best practice). No malicious behavior detected.
valueStatement: Need to create, encrypt, sign, or rotate cryptographic keys in Azure? This skill provides ready-to-use code snippets for the Azure Key Vault Keys SDK to handle key management securely.
limitations: Only supports Node.js runtime (browser not supported)
Requires Azure subscription and configured Key Vault
Does not support on-premises key management
Depends on network connectivity to Azure

useCases.title: Cloud Application Developer
useCases.description: Implement encryption for data at rest in cloud applications using Azure Key Vault for secure key storage and management.

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer
useCases.description: Manage key rotation policies and automate cryptographic key lifecycle operations in Azure environments.

useCases.title: Security Engineer
useCases.description: Implement proper key management workflows including backup, restore, and access controls for compliance.

promptTemplates.title: Create a new encryption key
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new RSA key named 'my-encryption-key' in Azure Key Vault with 2048-bit size. Enable the key and set an expiration date.
promptTemplates.scenario: Need to generate a new key for data encryption

promptTemplates.title: Encrypt data with managed key
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to encrypt a user password using the Azure Key Vault cryptography client with RSA-OAEP algorithm.

promptTemplates.title: Set up automatic key rotation
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure automatic rotation for 'my-rotation-key' so it rotates every 90 days. Show the rotation policy setup.

promptTemplates.title: Backup and restore a key
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I backup a key from one Azure Key Vault and restore it to a different vault? Include the backup and restore operations.

bestPractices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for automatic authentication across dev and production environments
Enable soft-delete on vaults to protect against accidental key deletion
Set expiration dates on keys and implement rotation policies for security compliance
antiPatterns: Hardcoding connection strings or secrets in source code - use Azure Key Vault or environment variables
Using keys without expiration dates - always set validity periods
Disabling key operations logging - audit trail is critical for security compliance

faq.question: What authentication methods does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill uses DefaultAzureCredential which supports managed identities, service principals, and developer credentials automatically.

faq.question: Can this skill work with on-premises key vaults?
faq.answer: No, Azure Key Vault is a cloud service. For on-premises, consider HashiCorp Vault or similar solutions.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with browser applications?
faq.answer: No, the Azure Key Vault SDK is Node.js only. Browser applications should use token-based authentication with a backend service.

faq.question: How do I handle key rotation in production?
faq.answer: Use the updateKeyRotationPolicy method to set automatic rotation. The rotateKey method can also be called manually or via automation.

faq.question: What key types are supported?
faq.answer: RSA (2048, 3072, 4096-bit), EC (P-256, P-384, P-521 curves), and AES symmetric keys are supported.

faq.question: Can I restore a key to a different vault?
faq.answer: Yes, the restoreKeyBackup method accepts backups from any vault, enabling cross-vault key migration.
